Repository: apache/dubbo-go mock

Project: apache/dubbo-go mock

Issue: Optimize URL parameter copying in common URL helpers

Issue Number: #4821

good first issue performance protocol common

Labels

  • good first issue
  • performance
  • protocol
  • common

Issue Body

Several common URL helpers copy the full parameter map even when the caller needs only a filtered or merged subset. The goal is to reduce allocation pressure without changing public behavior.

Expected Behavior

  • URL helpers preserve clone semantics for parameter maps.
  • MergeURL and CloneWithFilter return the same values as before.
  • Benchmarks show fewer allocations for common filter paths.

Current Behavior

  • MergeURL copies all parameters before filtering.
  • CloneWithFilter repeats map allocation in hot paths.
  • Existing tests cover values but not allocation behavior.

Suggested Implementation

  1. Inspect common/url.go and isolate parameter-copying helpers.
  2. Use targeted copying only for keys retained by MergeURL and CloneWithFilter.
  3. Add regression coverage in common/url_test.go.
  4. Add a benchmark that compares old and new allocation behavior.

Affected Modules

  • common/url.go
  • common/url_test.go

Maintainer Comments

  • Maintainer: benchmark is required before the patch can be reviewed.
  • Maintainer: preserve clone semantics so callers cannot mutate shared maps.
  • Related issue: MergeURL and CloneWithFilter should keep current behavior.

Linked PR / Related Files

  • Related draft PR: optimize-url-copying-mock
  • Related files: common/url.go, common/url_test.go

Acceptance Criteria

  • Existing URL helper tests pass.
  • New benchmark demonstrates reduced allocations.
  • Clone semantics are documented in tests.
  • No API or protocol behavior changes.

Risks

  • Changing copy order could accidentally share mutable maps.
  • Benchmark noise may hide small allocation improvements.
  • Protocol URL behavior must remain backward compatible.
